rationale: The citing paper references Huang and Zhang (2011) multiple times in its core conceptual framing, not only in a bibliography-style list. It explicitly uses the cited paper as evidence that progress velocity can change whether subgoal success leads to persistence versus licensing, and groups it with key work on situational moderators. This prior finding is then used to motivate the paper's main contribution: adding chronic individual differences (PLRM) beyond externally manipulated situational factors.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Huang and Zhang (2011) as part of the conceptual foundation showing that situational properties of subgoal success, especially perceived velocity, alter post-success motivation and behavior. It builds on that foundation by proposing and testing an additional, person-level moderator (PLRM) that explains divergent responses even under the same subgoal-success situation.
